seoTitle: Davenport vs Desert Springs — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Davenport and Desert Springs. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Davenport vs Desert Springs: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Davenport and Desert Springs.
heading: Davenport vs Desert Springs — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Davenport, Northern Territory and Desert Springs, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Davenport has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Desert Springs (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Davenport or Desert Springs safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Davenport has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Desert Springs scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Davenport scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Desert Springs scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Davenport or Desert Springs?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Davenport has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
